---
title: "CStar Host Convergence Backlog"
date: 2026-03-25
status: proposed
owner: "O.D.I.N."
---

# CStar Host Convergence Backlog

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This backlog exists to converge CStar's skills, weaves, and spells into executable authority.
> It also captures the Codex-local integration work adopted from CStar so the host layer stops drifting.

## Mission

The mission is to remove authority drift between lore, registry, runtime, and host integrations.

The required outcomes are:

1. `skill_registry.json` is the single source of truth for active capabilities.
2. Documentation becomes descriptive and never outranks runtime or registry truth.
3. Chant routing becomes registry-first instead of hardcoded-grammar-first.
4. Every active capability declares execution mode, verification, and host support.
5. Spells are explicitly classified as runtime-backed, policy-only, or deprecated.
6. Host support differences between Gemini, Codex, and Claude are declared, not implied.
7. The Codex-local skill and hook layer stays synchronized with CStar authority.

## Exit Criteria

- `scripts/audit_skill_registry.py` fails if any `ACTIVE` capability is missing execution authority, verification references, or host support metadata.
- `chant_parser.ts` does not maintain a second authoritative capability map separate from the registry.
- `AGENTS.qmd`, `AGENTS.md`, and related docs do not reference stale authority files or runtime semantics that the code does not implement.
- Host-session runtime tests continue to pass after host support normalization.
- Codex-local Corvus skills and hooks can be refreshed from CStar authority with one explicit sync path.

## Architectural Critique

The current risk is not conceptual weakness. It is ceremonial authority drift.

- The registry, chant router, host bridge, and tests already form a real control plane.
- The docs and taxonomy still overstate execution certainty in places.
- Spells are the highest-risk tier because they can read like executable runtime despite lacking uniform contracts.
- The chant parser still embeds a parallel closed grammar in code, which will drift unless the registry becomes the first resolver.
- Host parity is not real across Gemini, Codex, and Claude; pretending otherwise will produce fragile behavior.

This backlog therefore prioritizes registry authority, router hardening, verification linkage, and explicit host support over any expansion of lore.

## Ticket Index

| ID | Status | Priority | Ticket | Depends On | Primary Surfaces |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `CS-HC-01` | Proposed | P0 | Registry Authority Enrichment | none | registry, audit |
| `CS-HC-02` | Proposed | P0 | Documentation Authority Cleanup | `CS-HC-01` | AGENTS, docs |
| `CS-HC-03` | Proposed | P1 | Spell Classification Boundary | `CS-HC-01`, `CS-HC-02` | registry, AGENTS |
| `CS-HC-04` | Proposed | P0 | Chant Registry-First Routing | `CS-HC-01` | chant parser, chant runtime |
| `CS-HC-05` | Proposed | P1 | Tier-Specific Routing Semantics | `CS-HC-04` | chant parser, contracts |
| `CS-HC-06` | Proposed | P1 | Host Support Diagnostics | `CS-HC-01`, `CS-HC-04` | chant parser, host session |
| `CS-HC-07` | Proposed | P1 | Weave Contract Hardening | `CS-HC-01`, `CS-HC-04` | contracts, research, architect, distill, governor |
| `CS-HC-08` | Proposed | P1 | Host Governor Policy Externalization | `CS-HC-07` | governor, registry |
| `CS-HC-09` | Proposed | P2 | Start Resume Narrowing | `CS-HC-08` | adapters, operator resume |
| `CS-HC-10` | Proposed | P0 | Active Capability Verification Binding | `CS-HC-01` | registry, audit |
| `CS-HC-11` | Proposed | P1 | Host Bridge Contract Coverage | `CS-HC-06`, `CS-HC-07`, `CS-HC-10` | decoupling specs, bridge tests |
| `CS-HC-12` | Proposed | P0 | Registry-Declared Host Support | `CS-HC-01`, `CS-HC-10` | registry, host session, host intelligence, mimir |
| `CS-HC-13` | Proposed | P1 | Shared Control Plane Provider Cleanup | `CS-HC-12` | host intelligence, mimir, env adapter |
| `CS-HC-14` | Proposed | P1 | Codex Skill Sync Surface | `CS-HC-01`, `CS-HC-12` | ~/.codex sync, skills |
| `CS-HC-15` | Proposed | P2 | Codex Startup Grounding Upgrade | `CS-HC-14` | ~/.codex start hook |
| `CS-HC-16` | Proposed | P2 | Codex Shutdown Memory Upgrade | `CS-HC-14` | ~/.codex stop hook |
| `CS-HC-17` | Proposed | P1 | Explicit Codex Host Bridge | `CS-HC-12`, `CS-HC-14` | ~/.codex bridge/env |
| `CS-HC-18` | Proposed | P2 | Codex Drift Detection | `CS-HC-14`, `CS-HC-15`, `CS-HC-16`, `CS-HC-17` | ~/.codex manifest/sync |

## Critical Path

The minimum convergence path is:

`CS-HC-01 -> CS-HC-04 -> CS-HC-10 -> CS-HC-12 -> CS-HC-14`

`CS-HC-02` and `CS-HC-03` should land before broader documentation claims are expanded.

`CS-HC-07`, `CS-HC-08`, and `CS-HC-09` can proceed once registry authority and chant routing are stable.

`CS-HC-15` through `CS-HC-18` should follow after host-support declarations are stable enough to keep Codex in sync.

## Ticket Details

## `CS-HC-01` Registry Authority Enrichment

### Objective

Make the registry the single operational authority for active capabilities.

### Files

- `.agents/skill_registry.json`
- `scripts/audit_skill_registry.py`
- `docs/architecture/SKILL_REGISTRY.md`

### Change Set

- Add or normalize fields for `contracts`, `tests`, `owner_runtime`, `host_support`, and `recursion_policy`.
- Fail audit when an `ACTIVE` capability omits required authority fields.
- Document the new schema in `SKILL_REGISTRY.md`.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Every `ACTIVE` entry exposes execution authority and verification metadata.
- The audit script rejects incomplete active entries.

### Verification

```bash
python3 scripts/audit_skill_registry.py
python3 -m pytest tests/unit/test_skill_registry_audit.py -q
```

## `CS-HC-02` Documentation Authority Cleanup

### Objective

Demote docs to descriptive projections and purge stale references.

### Files

- `AGENTS.qmd`
- `AGENTS.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`
- `docs/HANDOFF.md`

### Change Set

- Remove or replace stale references such as `chant_upgrade_plan.json`.
- Clarify that registry and runtime outrank prose when behavior disagrees.
- Align tier descriptions with actual runtime capability.

### Acceptance Criteria

- No stale authority references remain.
- Documentation does not claim runtime behavior the code cannot prove.

### Verification

- Manual grep for stale file references.
- Targeted read-through of changed documents.

## `CS-HC-03` Spell Classification Boundary

### Objective

Stop presenting every spell as if it were an equal execution tier.

### Files

- `.agents/skill_registry.json`
- `AGENTS.qmd`
- `docs/architecture/SKILL_PERMUTATIONS.md`

### Change Set

- Tag each spell as `runtime-backed`, `policy-only`, or `deprecated`.
- Update docs to reflect the classification.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Spells no longer imply runtime authority without proof.

### Verification

```bash
python3 scripts/audit_skill_registry.py
python3 -m pytest tests/unit/test_skill_registry_audit.py -q
```

## `CS-HC-04` Chant Registry-First Routing

### Objective

Move chant capability routing to the registry-first model.

### Files

- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/chant_parser.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/chant.ts`

### Change Set

- Resolve direct capability routing from registry metadata before intent-category fallback.
- Reduce hardcoded category defaults to fallback status.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Chant does not depend on a parallel hardcoded capability map for primary resolution.

### Verification

```bash
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/node-runtime/weaves/test_chant_parser.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_chant_runtime.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_chant_planning_runtime.test.ts
```

## `CS-HC-05` Tier-Specific Routing Semantics

### Objective

Make tier semantics explicit in runtime contracts.

### Files

- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/chant_parser.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/contracts.ts`

### Change Set

- Encode how `PRIME/SKILL`, `WEAVE`, and `SPELL` may be routed.
- Prevent generic execution of `SPELL` entries unless marked `runtime-backed`.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Tier names imply one runtime meaning only.

### Verification

```bash
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/node-runtime/weaves/test_chant_parser.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/node-runtime/test_contracts.test.ts
```

## `CS-HC-06` Host Support Diagnostics

### Objective

Produce typed unsupported-host failures from declared metadata.

### Files

- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/chant_parser.ts`
- `src/core/host_session.ts`

### Change Set

- Use declared `host_support` to reject capabilities unsupported by the active provider.
- Surface typed failures rather than ad hoc missing-capability messages.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Unsupported host behavior is explicit and traceable to the registry.

### Verification

```bash
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_chant_runtime.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_host_session_weaves.test.ts
```

## `CS-HC-07` Weave Contract Hardening

### Objective

Make active weaves behave like bounded adapters rather than prompt envelopes.

### Files

- `src/node/core/runtime/contracts.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/architect.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/research.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/host_governor.ts`

### Change Set

- Tighten payload and result contracts for architect, research, distill, and governor-adjacent flows.
- Reject malformed host returns explicitly.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Weaves expose strict payload/result expectations.

### Verification

```bash
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_host_session_weaves.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_host_governor_runtime.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_distill_runtime.test.ts
```

## `CS-HC-08` Host Governor Policy Externalization

### Objective

Stop hiding boundedness and promotion rules in private constants.

### Files

- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/host_governor.ts`
- `.agents/skill_registry.json`

### Change Set

- Externalize governability thresholds or route them through declared policy metadata.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Promotion policy is auditable and testable.

### Verification

```bash
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_host_governor_runtime.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_start_runtime.test.ts
```

## `CS-HC-09` Start Resume Narrowing

### Objective

Keep `cstar start` a narrow wake-and-resume surface.

### Files

- `src/node/core/runtime/adapters.ts`
- `src/node/core/operator_resume.ts`

### Change Set

- Clarify and enforce `start` as the host-governed resume entrypoint rather than a generic overloaded command.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Start behavior is consistent across host-active and host-inactive execution.

### Verification

```bash
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_start_runtime.test.ts
```

## `CS-HC-10` Active Capability Verification Binding

### Objective

Make verification references mandatory for active capability records.

### Files

- `.agents/skill_registry.json`
- `scripts/audit_skill_registry.py`

### Change Set

- Require contract and unit-test references for `ACTIVE` capabilities.

### Acceptance Criteria

- No active capability can remain verification-opaque.

### Verification

```bash
python3 scripts/audit_skill_registry.py
python3 -m pytest tests/unit/test_skill_registry_audit.py -q
```

## `CS-HC-11` Host Bridge Contract Coverage

### Objective

Promote host bridge behavior into explicit contract coverage.

### Files

- `tests/empire_tests/specs/decoupling.qmd`
- `tests/crucible/contracts/bridge_protocol.qmd`

### Change Set

- Add or refine contract language for Gemini/Codex/Claude bridge behavior and decoupling expectations.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Bridge behavior exists at the contract layer, not just unit tests.

### Verification

```bash
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/empire_tests/test_ui_integration_empire.ts
python3 -m pytest tests/quarantine/test_decoupling_empire.py -q
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_host_session_weaves.test.ts
```

## `CS-HC-12` Registry-Declared Host Support

### Objective

Centralize provider support declarations.

### Files

- `.agents/skill_registry.json`
- `src/core/host_session.ts`
- `src/core/host_intelligence.ts`
- `src/core/mimir_client.ts`

### Change Set

- Consume `host_support` declarations in provider resolution and host invocation paths.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Host differences are declared centrally and enforced consistently.

### Verification

```bash
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_mimir_client_bridge.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_oracle_command.test.ts
```

## `CS-HC-13` Shared Control Plane Provider Cleanup

### Objective

Remove Gemini-only assumptions from shared host policy.

### Files

- `src/core/host_intelligence.ts`
- `src/core/mimir_client.ts`
- `src/core/engine/env_adapter.py`

### Change Set

- Replace provider-specific assumptions with declared provider capability logic.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Shared control-plane behavior is provider-aware instead of Gemini-biased.

### Verification

```bash
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_mimir_client_bridge.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsx.mjs --test tests/unit/test_host_session_weaves.test.ts
```

## `CS-HC-14` Codex Skill Sync Surface

### Objective

Keep Codex-local skills synchronized with CStar authority.

### Files

- `~/.codex/corvus/bin/sync_from_cstar.py`
- `~/.codex/corvus/README.md`
- `~/.codex/skills/corvus-grounding/SKILL.md`
- `~/.codex/skills/corvus-workflow-bridge/SKILL.md`
- `~/.codex/skills/cstar-runtime-bridge/SKILL.md`
- `~/.codex/skills/cstar-skill-registry/SKILL.md`

### Change Set

- Maintain a refresh path from CStar authority into the local Codex skill layer.

### Acceptance Criteria

- The Codex skill layer is refreshable instead of manually drifting.

### Verification

```bash
python3 ~/.codex/corvus/bin/sync_from_cstar.py --estate-root /home/morderith/Corvus
PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp python3 -m py_compile ~/.codex/corvus/bin/sync_from_cstar.py
```

## `CS-HC-15` Codex Startup Grounding Upgrade

### Objective

Expose the real runtime authority surfaces in Codex startup context.

### Files

- `~/.codex/corvus/hooks/session_start.py`

### Change Set

- Include registry, host bridge, governor, and local refresh-path context in startup grounding.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Codex startup context reflects current runtime authority, not just persona and hall guidance.

### Verification

```bash
python3 ~/.codex/corvus/hooks/session_start.py
```

## `CS-HC-16` Codex Shutdown Memory Upgrade

### Objective

Persist actual CStar workflow context in Codex session summaries.

### Files

- `~/.codex/corvus/hooks/session_stop.py`

### Change Set

- Capture `cstar` commands and Hall queries alongside normal command history.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Resume memory reflects real Corvus workflow usage.

### Verification

```bash
python3 ~/.codex/corvus/hooks/session_stop.py
```

## `CS-HC-17` Explicit Codex Host Bridge

### Objective

Provide a declared Codex bridge surface for CStar host execution.

### Files

- `~/.codex/corvus/bin/codex_host_bridge.py`
- `~/.codex/corvus/env/corvus-codex-env.sh`

### Change Set

- Keep Codex host execution behind one explicit bridge helper and env contract.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Codex support is explicit and routable from CStar without pretending Gemini-style directive parity.

### Verification

```bash
PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp python3 -m py_compile ~/.codex/corvus/bin/codex_host_bridge.py
```

## `CS-HC-18` Codex Drift Detection

### Objective

Detect when Codex-local integration no longer matches CStar authority.

### Files

- `~/.codex/corvus/bin/sync_from_cstar.py`
- `~/.codex/corvus/integration_manifest.json`

### Change Set

- Emit and compare a local authority snapshot for Codex skills/hooks.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Drift can be detected without manually rereading every local skill and hook.

### Verification

```bash
python3 ~/.codex/corvus/bin/sync_from_cstar.py --estate-root /home/morderith/Corvus
```

## Recommended First Slice

The smallest high-value implementation slice is:

1. `CS-HC-01`
2. `CS-HC-04`
3. `CS-HC-10`
4. `CS-HC-12`
5. `CS-HC-14`

That slice tightens authority, router behavior, verification binding, host support declarations, and Codex sync without expanding architecture first.
